title: Great Mosque of Córdoba
description: This quiz is for the essay Early period: The Great Mosque of Cordoba.
topic_path: Art of the Islamic world 640 to now

assessment_items.question_content:
**Prince Abd-al-Rahman I attempted to recreate in Spain the grandeur of . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Following the overthrow of his family (the Umayyads) in Damascus by the incoming Abbasids, Prince Abd al-Rahman I escaped to southern Spain. Once there, he established control over almost all of the Iberian Peninsula and attempted to recreate the grandeur of Damascus in his new capital, Córdoba.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/early-period-the-great-mosque-of-cordoba)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The architect of the Great Mosque of Córdoba borrowed the horseshoe arch from the [[☃ dropdown 1]] and was widely adopted into Western Islamic architecture.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f66f65d88fb67cf9aae75a4466b83355.png)
hints:
**The architect of the Great Mosque of Córdoba borrowed the horseshoe arch from the Visigoths and was widely adopted into Western Islamic architecture**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/early-period-the-great-mosque-of-cordoba)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Great Mosque of Córdoba?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54167d0ac34958af9a6809b321047d97.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**An iwan is a vaulted room that opens onto a courtyard. The Great Mosque of Córdoba does not have this feature.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/early-period-the-great-mosque-of-cordoba)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The mihrab is for a spiritual leader to stand in and address the congregation.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39d5fe31199e8ac1399d8b68bf54e00e.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**A mihrab is used in a mosque to identify the wall that faces Mecca—the birth place of Islam in what is now Saudi Arabia. This is practical as Muslims face toward Mecca during their daily prayers.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/early-period-the-great-mosque-of-cordoba)**
